name: Shu Bow (Pork Buns)
recipeInstructions:
BUNS
1. In a bowl, dissolve yeast in warm water. Blend in sugar, oil, and salt. Let stand in a warm place until bubbly (about 15 minutes).
2. Gradually add flour and mix until dough holds together. Place dough on a lightly floured board and knead until smooth and elastic (8-10 minutes).
3. Place dough in a greased bowl, cover, and let rise in a warm place until doubled in bulk (about 1 hour 15 minutes).
PORK FILLING
4. In a large bowl, cut pork into 1/2-inch cubes. Season with minced garlic, grated ginger, 2 tablespoons sugar, and 2 tablespoons Chinese soy sauce.
5. In a separate bowl, combine 2 teaspoons sugar, cornstarch, 2 tablespoons soy sauce, dry sherry, and water; reserve.
6. Heat oil in a wok or wide frying pan over high heat. When oil is hot, add pork and stir-fry until browned (about 5 minutes). Add chopped onion and continue to stir fry until onion is limp (about 2 minutes).
7. Stir in cornstarch mixture and cook with pork until sauce thickens and bubbles.
8. Shape:
- Turn dough out onto a lightly floured board.
- Knead for 1 minute.
- Cut into rectangle, then cut crosswise 6 times to make 12 equal pieces.
- Roll each piece into a round about 4-1/2 inches in diameter.
- Press outside edges of dough to make them slightly thinner than the rest of the round.
9. Place about 2 tablespoons of filling in center of each round. Fold and twist edges of dough around filling, sealing well.
10. Place each bun on a square of foil (3 inches). Cover and let rise in a warm place until puffy and light (about 30 minutes).
11. Steam: Set buns in steamer over boiling water. Cover and steam for 12-15 minutes. Tops should be glazed and smooth when done.
Serve warm.
